# Title: Invoice requirements for appealing a policy violation

Title: Invoice requirements for appealing a policy violation

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GDQ9K277NYP6WNEW

Markdown Content:
Watch [Respond to a Product Quality and Authenticity performance notification](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/learn/courses?ref_=su_course_accordion&moduleId=5df5bcdd-328e-43f7-b400-8178d45dd550&courseId=b1a08bc8-bca0-47a1-8ce0-8e5615ab2f98&modLanguage=English&videoPlayer=youtube) to identify the policy violation that requires an invoice as a part of your appeal.

## What is an Invoice?

An invoice is a document that records the final transaction of goods between a buyer and a supplier. It is issued directly by a supplier and shows an itemized list of goods received and the terms of sale for a completed transaction.

![Image 1](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/G/01/generic/invoiceimg.png)[](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GDQ9K277NYP6WNEW)

## Why are Invoices required for Amazon?

## Why are Invoices required for Amazon?

To address many of Amazon’s policy violations, you must submit copies of invoices from your suppliers. Amazon requires invoices to verify that sellers are sourcing their products from legitimate suppliers, which helps maintain trust in the marketplace. Clear invoice documentation helps prevent listing removals, supports account health, and speeds up appeal resolutions.

## What should the invoice include?

Invoices must include:

Supplier Information*   Contact information of your supplier, including a name, phone number, address, and website. We won’t share this information with our retail team or other Amazon sellers.

_Note: For information on sourcing suppliers, see ‘How can I source a verified supplier?’_ FAQ at the bottom of this page.
Transaction Details*   A clearly displayed issue date within the past 365 days before the receipt of the performance notification.
*   Quantity sufficient to cover your sales volumes and in-stock inventory for each ASIN cited in the performance notification over the past 365 days.
*   Sales records for each of the ASIN associated with the policy violation.

_Note: If your invoice shows multiple items, you may highlight the relevant product for your appeal_
Product Information*   Clear product identifiers; this includes product names, model numbers, or universal product identifiers (UPC, EAN, or ISBN).
*   For product variations, include specific details (version, color, or style) matching your listing
*   For bundles, list all individual components included in your listing
Document Standards*   The invoice must be in PDFs (.pdf) or image files (.jpg, .png, or .gif) format, and no larger than 10MB.

Other important considerations for your invoices:

Other important considerations for your invoices:

*   The invoice must document a completed and fulfilled transaction
*   There may be additional regional requirements (that is, state/province tax information), which will be provided to sellers on our emails.
*   Any document you provide must be authentic and unaltered.
*   You can remove pricing information from the invoices, but make sure that all other information is visible and clear.
*   We might reach out to the supplier to verify your documents information and will always keep your suppliers’ information confidential.

## What won’t be accepted?

The following will **not**be accepted for review:

## What won’t be accepted?

The following will **not**be accepted for review:

*   Supplier invoices sent by email or via third-party sharing; they must be submitted directly through the Seller Central.
*   Sales quotes, pro forma invoices, or any document that is sent in advance of a finalized invoice or completed transaction.
*   Self-issued invoices or invoices where the supplier appears to be the same business entity as the buyer.
*   Invoices that have been digitally manipulated or are in an editable format such as .xlsx (Excel) or .docx (Word)
*   Invoices that are illegible, low resolution, show inconsistent information, or are missing required details.

**Note:**We may reject your invoices if they do not meet our requirements or if we otherwise evaluate that your invoices are inauthentic or inaccurate. If we reject an appeal that included invoices, refer to your Performance Notifications that will identify any reasons why we could not accept the invoices you provided.

## How to submit an Invoice as part of an appeal

To submit an invoice as part of an appeal, follow the instructions in the banner at the top of your [Account Health](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/performance/dashboard) dashboard.

## FAQ

**How can I source a verified supplier?**

When evaluating new suppliers, consider the following verification methods:

*   Review official business documentation, such as business licenses.
*   Verify a physical business presence, such as an office or warehouse location.
*   Check business credibility through business bureau ratings, company background, media coverage, industry reputation, and online presence.
*   Verify web presence through government resources, brand websites, business directories, and other credible sources.

**Does the invoice have to be in English (are other languages accepted)?**

No, it is not required that your invoice is submitted in English.

**Does the invoice have to be in English (are other languages accepted)?**

No, it is not required that your invoice is submitted in English.

**Is there alternative documentation needed for media products (books, CDs, etc)?**

Media products follow the same documentation requirements as all other items.
